article.title: Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety: Customizable Multimodal Safety for Global Enterprise AI

harvestedTerms.summary: Nvidia releases Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety, a multimodal, multilingual enterprise safety classifier built on Gemma 3 4B with custom policy enforcement, auditable reasoning traces (THINK mode), and an open safety dataset. The article is primarily a product announcement but introduces several technically distinct patterns in AI safety infrastructure.

article.title: An Interview with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella About Finding Core Competencies

harvestedTerms.summary: A Stratechery interview with Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella covering Microsoft's competitive repositioning, the MAI model line, the OpenAI partnership, Project Solara, and the conceptual framing of AI as a 'learning machine' and 'hill-climbing machine' enabling firms to build their own token capital infrastructure. Key novel framings: the multi-tenant learning system, token capital as a new firm resource, and clean-lineage model development.

mustReadFlagged.reason: A rare primary-source interview in which a major AI platform CEO articulates a new conceptual model — 'token capital,' 'hill-climbing machines,' clean-lineage models — for how firms should think about AI as a factor of production. More substantive than a product-launch interview; Nadella is visibly working out a new strategic framework in real time.

article.title: A quote from Emanuel Maiberg, 404 Media

harvestedTerms.summary: A short blog post quoting Emanuel Maiberg (404 Media) on Google quietly asking to retract a public statement affirming 'humans in the loop' in AI systems. The substance is thin — it's a single quoted observation with minimal surrounding analysis — but the underlying story points to a meaningful tension between public AI safety messaging and corporate backpedaling on human oversight commitments.

article.title: Kevin O’Leary agrees to downsize massive Utah data center

harvestedTerms.summary: Kevin O'Leary agrees to reduce his planned 40,000-acre Project Stratos data center in Utah by roughly half following community and political pressure, though the remaining ~20,000 acres still exceeds the size of Manhattan. The article briefly surfaces concerns about energy usage, water consumption, and environmental impact of large-scale AI infrastructure.

article.title: Your Company Needs an Energy Strategy for AI’s Next Phase

harvestedTerms.summary: HBR article arguing that the key AI competitive constraint is shifting from model access and GPUs to electricity and energy infrastructure, framing energy strategy as the next frontier for enterprise AI.
